title: Goniopteris heterophlebia
intro: Goniopteris heterophlebia is a species of fern native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Goniopteris heterophlebia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Goniopteris heterophlebia is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Goniopteris heterophlebia is classified within the plant kingdom as a member of the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns. The genus Goniopteris is also part of this family, and it is a group of ferns native to tropical regions. The exact classification of Goniopteris heterophlebia may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized within these broader categories.
